California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Edward Duddy 
Moreno Valley city Attorney 
Zimmer & Singer, Inc. 
P.O. Box 788 
Riverside, CA 
92502 
Dear Mr. Duddy: 
May 9, 1986 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. I-86-153 
Your letter has been received. 
Pursuant to Government Code 
section 83114 and Regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code section 
l8329(b) (8) (A) and (c) (7) (A), we decline to provide you or 
Mr. Webb with advice regarding the propriety of past conduct. 
Actions which have occurred in the past either were violations 
of the law or were not, dependent upon all of the material 
facts. 
The courts or the administrative enforcement procedure, 
with the attendant due process rights, would be the appropriate 
forums for determining the facts under such circumstances, not 
an advice request. 
Should you or Mr. Webb desire prospective advice regarding 
future conduct, please do not hesitate to contact this office 
in advance. 
REL:plh 
Enclosure 
sincerely, 
r'7?1 j 
.::' 
~/;Z// 
,>!,~ /. 
? 
.) 
0~' 
(' 
/ 
. <-
Robert E. L~igh 
Counsel 
Legal Division 


DONALD F". ZIMMER 
M. NEAL SINGER 
EDWARD DUDDY 
Legal Division 
LAW OrFICES 
ZIMMER & SINGER, IN!=!. e 
4298 ORANGE STREET 
P.O.BOX788 
lit 1\ 
RIVERSIDE, CALIFORNIA 9258ift (. 
April 18, 1986 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street, Suite 800 
Post Office Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804-0807 
Re: 
Request for Formal Written Advice 
Gentlemen: 
p 
Aa.l ~~"&LE:PHONE: 784-0500 
~ 16 Kll 
AREA CODr: 714 
I am the City Attorney for the City of Moreno Valley. 
I am writing to you with the authorization of Mr. Stephen 
Webb, a Councilmember of the City of Moreno Valley, to 
request your written advice regarding whether Mr. Webb was 
disqualified from voting on a matter which came before the 
City Council on January 21, 1986, January 28, 1986, February 
11, 1986, and February 18, 1986. 
Acting on my oral and written advice as City Attorney, 
Mr. Webb participated in the questioned matter. I have 
enclosed a copy of my February 12, 1986 written ,memorandum 
advice on the issue, together with the attachments to that 
memorandum. 
As you will note, the written advice merely 
confirms prior oral advice given to Mr. Webb on February 7, 
1986 and to the City Council on February 11, 1986. 
I have also enclosed a copy of a February 15, 1986 
letter from Mr. George Hague, a citizen of Moreno Valley, 
disputing Mr. Webb's right to participate in the City 
Council's deliberations on the questioned matter. 
The 
letter is addressed "To Whom It May Concern." 
The 
attachments to Mr. Hague's letter have been deleted as 
being, except for a copy of a newspaper article, duplicates 
of my February 12, 1986 advice memorandum which is enclosed 
herewith. 
The newspaper article is a report of the part of 
the February 11, 1986 meeting at which the City Council 
considered the matter at issue. 
I have set forth below the facts which appear to me to 
be relevant to the question regarding Mr. Webb's 
qualification .to participate in the City Council's 
deliberations on the matter. 


Legal Division 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
April 18, 1986 
Page 2 
QUESTION PRESENTED: 
A City Councilmember'of the City of Moreno Valley, 
possesses a financial interest, within the meaning of 
Government Code 87103(c) and 87103(d), in a company which 
provides cable television services within the City. 
Does 
possession of such an interest create a conflict of interest 
such as to disqualify Mr. Webb from participation in City 
Council consideration of a project reasonably expected to 
generate new subscribers to cable television services 
generally? 
SUMMARY OF PERTINENT FACTS: 
Mr. Stephen Webb is a Councilmember of the City of 
Moreno Valley. 
Mr. Webb is employed by Moreno Cablevision 
("Cablevision") in a managerial position and received more 
than $250 from Cablevision in each of the respective 12-
month periods prior to January 21, 1986, January 28, 1986, 
February 11, 1986, and February 18, 1986. 
Mr. Webb has no 
ownership interest in Cablevision. 
On January 21, 1986, January 28, 1986, February 11, 
1986 and February 18, 1986, Mr. Webb participated in City 
Council deliberations or voted, or both, on three 
development-related proposals submitted on behalf of Griffin 
Homes. 
The proposals included a specific plan and 
subdivision under which 1,214 residential units would be 
developed on approximately 483 acres over a contemplated 
period of time of ten years. 
The other two proposals were 
for a change of zone to accommodate the proposal and an 
annexation/development agreement, pursuant to which the 
specific plan would be implemented. 
On January 21, 1986, Mr. Webb voted with the other four 
members of the City Council to continue consideration of the 
proposals to January 28, 1986. 
On January 28, 1986, in a 
series of three to two votes, Mr. Webb voted in favor of 
approving the specific plan, to introduce a rezoning 
ordinance, to approve the related tentative tract maps, and 
to continue until February 11, 1986, consideration of the 
proposed development agreement. 
On February 11, 1986, the 
development agreement was approved and on February 18, 1986, 
an ordinance was adopted, approving that agreement, both on 
three to two votes with Mr. Webb voting in favor. 
The 483 acres involved in the subject proposals were 
formerly part of a different development owned by a 


Legal Division 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
April 18, 1986 
Page 3 
different developer (Markborough), and as to which a 
different already-approved specific plan applied. 
The 
Markborough specific plan 'contemplated development of 461 
residential units on the 483 acres which became part of the 
Specific Plan under which Griffin Homes proposed the 
development of 1,214 residential units. 
Prior to January 21, 1986, then-existing zoning for the 
483 acres would have permitted the development of 783 
residential units, as follows: 
(a) 145 acres in the R-A-2 1/2 zone, allowing 58 units; 
(b) 60 acres in the W-2-20 zone, allowing 3 units; 
(c) 99.5 developable acres (out of 132.5) in the R-4 
zone, allowing 721 units; and 
(d) 146.5 acres in the R-5 zone (open-space-
combination, allowing no residential units. 
The Markborough specific plan and zoning were approved by 
the Riverside County Board of Supervisors prior to the 1984 
incorporation of the City. 
The actions in which Mr. Webb 
participated resulted in increasing the 783 allowable number 
of residential units under prior zoning by 431 to the 1,214 
units approved for the Griffin Homes specific plan and 
zoning. 
Mr. Webb's company, Moreno Cablevision, is one of two 
cable television service companies presently serving the 
City of Moreno Valley (under currently applicable Federal 
law, franchises for such businesses may be required by the 
City, but such franchises may not be exclusive). 
The 
transfer of ownership interests in Cablevision is permitted 
by California law within the meaning of Section 
18702.2(e)(1) of the regulations of the Fair Political 
Practices Commission (2 Cal.Adm.Code, Section 18702.2). 
There is, and has been, no agreement between 
Cablevision and the developer pursuant to which Cablevision 
would be given any right or preference in respect to cable 
television service to all or any part of the development. 
Also, Mr. Webb has informed me that the development is large 
enough that it could feasibly provide its own cable 
television system, one which would not be subject to the 
City's franchise requirements. 


Legal Division 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
April 18, 1986 
Page 4 
Mr. Webb has informed me further that Cablevision has a 
historical penetration rate of 17.8 percent of the 
residences in the City, and an average monthly billing of 
$23.00 per customer. 
This penetration rate and average 
billing figures applied at all times pertinent to the 
actions in question in this letter. 
If you have any question regarding this letter, please let 
me know. 
Mr. Webb may be reached by telephone at 
(714) 242-6651 or by mail at City of Moreno Valley, Post 
Office Box 1440, Moreno Valley, California. 
ED/pd 
Enclosures 
cc: 
Councilmember Webb 
Mayor Nieburger 
Councilmember Horspool 
Councilmember Lynn 
Councilmember Scott 
City Manager Dixon 
Very truly yours, 
ULJ ~;:j() 
Edward Duddy, City Attorney 
City of Moreno Valley

428 J Street, Suite 800 (P. O. Box 807) 
Sacramento, California 95804-0807 
TELEPHONE 764-0500 
AREA CODE 714 
Re: 
Request for Formal written ~dvice (S. Webb) -
Correction 
Gentlemen: 
Please refer to my letter of April 18, 1986, requesting 
your written advice as to whether or not Councilmember 
Stephen Webb of the City of Moreno Valley was disqualifed 
from participating in a matter which came before the City 
Council. 
The statement in the second paragraph on page 2 of my 
April 18th letter, to the effect that Mr. Webb has no 
ownership interest in Cablevision, should be deleted. 
Mr. Webb does in fact own a minority interest in Cablevision 
acquired in mid-1985. 
This correction should not affect 
the answer to the question presented, inasmuch as Mr. Webb's 
employment already has established a financial interest in 
Cablevision on the part of Mr. Webb. 
Please telephone me at the above number if you have any 
questions. 
Very truly yours, 
ZIMMER & SINGER, INC. 
~~ 
Edward Duddy, City Attorney 
ED/h 
City of Moreno Valley

City Manager Dixon

Council consideration of a project reasonably expected to 
generate new subscribers to cable television services 
generally?

different developer (Markborough), and as to which a

Box 788 Riverside, CA 92502 Dear Mr. Duddy: May 9, 1986 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. I-86-153 Your letter has been received. Pursuant to Government Code section 83114 and Regulation 2 Cal. Adm. Code section l8329(b) (8) (A) and (c) (7) (A), we decline to provide you or Mr. Webb with advice regarding the propriety of past conduct. Actions which have occurred in the past either were violations of the law or were not, dependent upon all of the material facts. The courts or the administrative enforcement procedure, with the attendant due process rights, would be the appropriate forums for determining the facts under such circumstances, not an advice request. Should you or Mr. Webb desire prospective advice regarding future conduct, please do not hesitate to contact this office in advance. April 18, 1986 Fair Political Practices Commission 428 J Street, Suite 800 Post Office Box 807 Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 Re: Request for Formal Written Advice Gentlemen: p Aa.l ~~"&LE:PHONE: 784-0500 ~ 16 Kll AREA CODr: 714 I am the City Attorney for the City of Moreno Valley. I am writing to you with the authorization of Mr. Stephen Webb, a Councilmember of the City of Moreno Valley, to request your written advice regarding whether Mr. Webb was disqualified from voting on a matter which came before the City Council on January 21, 1986, January 28, 1986, February 11, 1986, and February 18, 1986. Acting on my oral and written advice as City Attorney, Mr. Webb participated in the questioned matter. I have enclosed a copy of my February 12, 1986 written ,memorandum advice on the issue, together with the attachments to that memorandum. As you will note, the written advice merely confirms prior oral advice given to Mr. Webb on February 7, 1986 and to the City Council on February 11, 1986. I have also enclosed a copy of a February 15, 1986 letter from Mr. George Hague, a citizen of Moreno Valley, disputing Mr. Webb's right to participate in the City Council's deliberations on the questioned matter. The letter is addressed "To Whom It May Concern." The attachments to Mr. Hague's letter have been deleted as being, except for a copy of a newspaper article, duplicates of my February 12, 1986 advice memorandum which is enclosed herewith. The newspaper article is a report of the part of the February 11, 1986 meeting at which the City Council considered the matter at issue. I have set forth below the facts which appear to me to be relevant to the question regarding Mr. Webb's qualification .to participate in the City Council's deliberations
